info.name: Attendify Administrative API
info.description:
Administrative workspace endpoints for Attendify.

Frontend auth flow for this collection:
1. `POST /api/administrative/login`
2. Save `response.data.data.token` and `response.data.data.user`
3. `GET /api/administrative/me`
4. If the account is a promoted doctor, inspect `administrative_access` and `available_workspaces`; the same token can access both doctor and administrative workspaces
5. `POST /api/administrative/devices/token` if Firebase push is enabled
6. Use the bearer token on all later requests
7. `DELETE /api/administrative/devices/token` before `POST /api/administrative/logout`

New administrative access is granted to doctor accounts through `administrative_access=true`; direct creation of new standalone administrative accounts is disabled. Legacy standalone administrative accounts may still exist and remain supported.

item.name: Auth

request.description:
Authenticate into the administrative workspace.

Request body:
- `email`
- `password`

Save the returned token from `response.data.data.token`, save the user object from `response.data.data.user`, then call `GET /api/administrative/me`. If the same account also has doctor access, the profile exposes `available_workspaces` and the same token can be reused for both workspaces.
Use this request immediately after login and on app start to verify the saved administrative token and inspect `administrative_access` plus `available_workspaces`.

Register or refresh the Firebase device token for the current administrative session.

Call this immediately after a successful login and again whenever the FCM token changes.

Remove the Firebase device token for the current administrative session.

Call this before logout when push notifications are enabled, then call the normal logout endpoint and clear local storage.
If push is enabled, call `DELETE /api/administrative/devices/token` first with the current FCM token, then call logout and clear local storage.

item.description:
Authentication runtime for administrative clients:
1. Login with `email` and `password`
2. Save `response.data.data.token`
3. Call `GET /api/administrative/me`
4. Inspect `administrative_access` and `available_workspaces`
5. Register Firebase token with `POST /api/administrative/devices/token`
6. Reuse the same token if the same account also opens the doctor workspace
7. Remove the device token before logout

request.description: Update college-level attendance settings. excuse_receiver accepts administrative or doctor, and qr_rotation_seconds controls QR token rotation and expiry.
Create a college broadcast. When `target=doctors`, the announcement is delivered to all doctor accounts in the current college.
Lists excuses for this college. Each excuse now includes all uploaded attachments, including legacy single-file excuses and new attachments[].
Review an excuse only when this college routes excuses to the administrative office. resolution values: excused_permission, excused_exemption, keep_absent.
Directly update a single attendance record. Allowed statuses are present, absent, late, permitted, and exempted.
Returns attendance analytics with separate counts for present, absent, late, permitted, exempted, and the aggregate excused_total.
term_id must belong to the same college and selected level. Use the majors endpoint to inspect levels and the subject/exam helper endpoints to resolve valid academic ids before creating records.

Use a real attendance record id from the attendance report endpoints. The status must be one of the editable attendance states configured by the backend, such as present, absent, late, permitted, or exempted.
Use create-data and helper endpoints first to resolve a valid major_id, level_id, term_id, and subject_id combination. A hard-coded term outside the selected level will correctly fail validation.

body.raw:
{
  "major_id": {{major_id}},
  "level_id": {{level_id}},
  "term_id": {{term_id}},
  "title": "Midterm Schedule Updated",
  "description": "Updated spring exam schedule",
  "is_published": true,
  "items": [
    {
      "subject_id": {{subject_id}},
      "exam_date": "2026-05-03",
      "start_time": "10:00",
      "end_time": "12:00",
      "location": "Hall B"
    }
  ]
}

request.description: Create an academic schedule row. hall_name is optional and can be null.
Update an academic schedule row. hall_name remains optional.
